description_long: This is a delicate flowering plant with a tall, slender, and slightly hairy stem that supports multiple small, star-shaped white flowers. The flowers have five elongated petals, with two upper petals and three lower petals, the lower central one being the largest and often tinged with pink and yellow at the base. The plant features several large, round, dark green leaves with prominent veins and slightly serrated edges, growing close to the base. Fine roots are visible at the bottom, and the overall structure is airy and open, with flowers spaced along the upper portion of the stem. The flowers are arranged in a loose, branching inflorescence, giving the plant a graceful, upright appearance. This type of plant is commonly found in shaded urban parks, gardens, or as ornamental vegetation along sidewalks.
description: A tall, slender plant with star-shaped white flowers and large, round, dark green leaves clustered at the base. The flowers are arranged in a loose, branching pattern along the upper stem.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the plant, with the round leaves and roots clearly visible at the base and the star-shaped flowers spaced along the upright stem.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical stem and the arrangement of flowers, with the leaves partially obscured and the branching inflorescence more apparent.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing the underside of the leaves and the back of the flowers, with the pink and yellow tinges on the petals more visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the leaves and roots visible from a different angle, and the flowers' orientation along the stem clearly displayed.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a bilateral configuration, with two upper petals and three lower petals, the central lower petal being the largest. The petals are free, not fused, and display slight asymmetry with some overlapping at the base.
